description: Lecture 9.2 discusses the extension of longest chain consensus using proof-of-work civil resistance in permissionless settings. It reviews key concepts from the previous lecture and outlines the differences in leader selection and block proposal processes in this context.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the transition from permissioned to permissionless blockchain consensus, focusing on proof-of-work civil resistance and its implications for leader selection and block proposals. Key differences from previous models are highlighted, including the event-driven nature of rounds and the limitations on block proposals.
